title: Super-potent synthetic opioids called nitazenes are spreading across the U.S.
summary: Overdose deaths confirmed to involve nitazenes have risen sharply in the U.S., from 27 in 2020 to 409 in 2024, according to the CDC

title: Opinion: Congress should embrace strategic health diplomacy
summary: “Improving global health should be a top policy priority of the United States,” write Anand Parekh, Tom Daschle, and Bill Frist.

title: What’s next for GLP-1 weight loss drugs?
summary: What’s next for GLP-1 weight loss drugs? Alex Hogan explores on the new episode of STATus Report. Watch now.

title: STAT+: Senate Democrats demand HHS provide records on federal vaccine policy
summary: Senate Democrats open an inquiry into the Trump administration’s remaking of U.S. vaccine policy, demanding officials produce records by next week.

title: New infant botulism outbreak puts fancy formulas under scrutiny
summary: A new infant botulism outbreak puts fancy formulas that position their products as safer than other options under scrutiny.

title: Opinion: AI’s growing appetite for power is a public health issue. Here’s a fix
summary: “AI is creating a new and largely overlooked strain on something fundamental to health,” write Sten H. Vermund and Patricia J. Kissinger.

title: Kardigan extends biotech’s streak of big IPOs with $400M haul
summary: Including the heart drug developer’s offering, four biotechs in 2026 have raised at least $400 million in IPO proceeds — the most in a single year since 2021, according to BioPharma Dive data.

title: RFK Jr. presents $700 million in mental health funding, but experts say grants aren’t new
summary: RFK Jr. presents funding intended to address mental illness and addiction, particularly among homeless people.

title: STAT+: DOJ’s swift win in OhioHealth case should have hospitals studying their contracts, experts say
summary: The speed of DOJ's proposed antitrust settlement with OhioHealth should put other hospitals on notice, experts say.

title: FTC, four state AGs sue transgender health group over care standards
summary: The FTC and four state attorneys general have sued the main professional organization for gender-affirming care clinicians.

title: NIH diversity programs doubled undergraduates’ odds of getting a Ph.D., 20-year study finds
summary: A new study found that two diversity-oriented programs supported by the NIH doubled the odds that an undergraduate would earn a Ph.D.

title: Luigi Mangione will assert psychiatric defense in murder case in UnitedHealthcare CEO’s killing
summary: Luigi Mangione plans to assert a psychiatric defense at his state murder trial, claiming he was suffering from extreme emotional disturbance when he gunned down UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

title: UniQure to file gene therapy for approval, reflecting major shifts at FDA
summary: The decision marks another regulatory U-turn following the exits of Marty Makary and Vinay Prasad, suggesting to some analysts that current FDA leadership may be more flexible in certain cases.

title: STAT+: Advocacy groups petition FDA to withdraw approval of ‘unsafe’ use of antibiotics given to livestock
summary: More than 60 organizations are urging the FDA to withdraw approval for what they called the “unsafe” use of antibiotics for preventing disease in food-producing livestock.

title: Jazz zeroes in on next-gen T cell engagers with AbCellera deal
summary: The potentially $4 billion collaboration is the latest example of drugmakers working to broaden the use of T cell engagers, which, so far, have had limited success outside of certain blood cancers.

title: A new Westlake-backed biotech takes aim at migraines

title: Express Scripts, PCMA sue to block Tennesee law breaking up PBMs and pharmacies
summary: Express Scripts and the PBM lobby are following in CVS Caremark’s footsteps in filing complaints challenging the FAIR Rx Act, which was passed earlier this year despite vehement opposition from PBMs.

summary: Ahead of a Thursday advisory panel meeting, agency scientists highlighted shortcomings in data accrued for a vaccine the FDA controversially refused to even review earlier this year.

summary: Still, the results leave somewhat unclear how different the company’s drug is than marketed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy medicines from Bristol Myers Squibb and Cytokinetics.
